title: Subjunctive in adverbial clauses
objectives: Use the subjunctive after time, purpose, and condition connectors
Know which connectors switch between subjunctive and indicative
grammar_notes: ALWAYS subjunctive: antes de que, para que, sin que, a menos que, con tal de que.

Time connectors (cuando, hasta que, en cuanto): SUBJUNCTIVE if the action is in the future, INDICATIVE if it's a fact or habitual. 'Cuando llegues, llámame' (future) vs 'Cuando llego a casa, ceno' (habit).
Russian 'когда' doesn't switch mood, but 'чтобы' (purpose) does take a special form — closest parallel to Spanish 'para que + subjunctive'.
example_phrases: Cuando vengas, te enseñaré la casa.
Voy a esperar hasta que llegue.
